Young MRC - Young Model Railway Club.
A monthly Saturday morning club for 8-14 year old modellers
Young MRC is the monthly Saturday morning club for younger railway modellers, aged from 8 to 14, which is held from 10am to 12 noon at Keen House.
-Help to build a layout
-Improve your skills
-Meet other young modellers and swap know-how
-Run your trains on our layout or large test tracks
-And for the grown ups: advice to help build your own layout at home from some of the MRC's experts.
Adults: Our club nights are every Thursday evening from 7pm, when visitors are welcome. Sometimes we have guest Speakers (see the What's On page of this website). Our exhibition layouts are not normally erected in working order as they are often being worked on in sections. However, members often run their trains around the test tracks that we have in the club room. Visitors are more than welcome to come and have a chat, a drink and watch the trains!

full_address: Wormwood Scrubs RDA is situated in West London. The Centre caters for children and young people with physical disabilities and learning difficulties. During the week children from local special needs schools come to the Centre for riding and other forms of equine therapy. On Saturdays individual children attend as well as out of school groups. Our ponies can carry up to 9st in weight. The Centre has an indoor arena and a unique stable yard therapy classroom which facilitates many learning tools and a dressage horse simulator. The centre has in the region of 60 volunteers who kindly help in many different ways. All volunteers who wish to help with the ponies and riders are given necessary training. If you are interested in booking in a school group or paying a visit to the centre please send an email.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Cherie Ade(5): I have been struggling to find a good riding school that allows me to wear the safety gear I wanted. I almost gave up until I found Barnfield! Not only were they happy with it they encouraged saftey too! Another thing that blew me away about this riding school is that they really care about their clients enjoying their riding experience and I really appreciated this. Encouragement and a positive nature as well as constructive feedback is what makes a quality instructor and I felt this while riding at Barnfield. I'm always keen to learn from stables about the horse experience and Barnfield is one of the few places I went to that seemed invested in this so it is one of the things that will keep me coming back. The last thing I want to say is that they were very welcoming to me and treated me like a person, and I know this seems a very basic thing to say but good customer service goes a long way! Some places I've been to treated me like I wasn't worth the time of day because they are too busy. Barnfield is a busy school, but they still take time to smile and welcome you and let you know where you stand and where you need to go next/ what you need to do, and that is really important. Thank you everyone at Barnfield Riding school! Xxx | Ed(5): I have been to Barnfield riding school with my partner every week for the past two years. We have always had a great time and we want to thank Patsy and her staff for always being helpful and patient with us, not least special thanks to the horses and ponies that we ride including Ameera, Candy, Mr. T, Tessie, Jazz, Sienna, Star and others! | Suzanne Gilmour(5): I have been riding at Barnfield for some time. It is a very professional, welcoming Riding School where the horses & ponies receive consistent, high quality care plus a lot of love! There are enough different instructors so you can find the one that suits you, or chop & change as you wish. A holistic approach to riding is offered with a mixture of lessons in the arena, on the mechanical horse & out hacking. All clients are encouraged to interact with the horses to widen their knowledge. I recommend Barnfield to anyone who loves horses, & wants a hands-on approach to learning to ride.

description_full:
Braywick Heath Nurseries,
Maidenhead. SL6 1DX
https://www.theautismgroup.org.uk/clubs/
Our Special Interest Clubs are built around topics that young people told us they enjoy. You can find clubs offering Pokemon, Arts and Crafts and Console Gaming plus our Saturday morning starter club for ages 9 to 13 called The GAP Club which combines popular activities from each of our other clubs. Run by qualified and DBS checked leaders and volunteers, sessions run on Saturdays throughout the year in the Maidenhead area.
We cater for young people aged between 9 and 25 who have a diagnosis of an autistic spectrum condition and are typically of mainstream ability, but need support to succeed in social situations.
Our Clubs are:
The GAP Club for ages 9 to 13.
Pokemon Club for ages 11 to 25.
Fusion Club for ages 11 to 25
Arts and Crafts for ages 11 to 25.
Console Gaming for ages 16 to 25.

name: Shintaido Class - Reading
description_full:
Improve non-verbal communication and physical co-ordination.
Shintaido is a modern movement system based on traditional Japanese martial arts with the emphasis on self-development and artistic-expression rather than self-defence. It offers direct training in non-verbal communication helps with co-ordination and can lead to other positive life changes over time.
AutAngel is running community Shintaido sessions; open to all. Charles Burns, our experienced and qualified instructor, has Asperger’s Syndrome and has found Shintaido enormously helpful in overcoming various problems associated with this condition.

It was rife with bullying, girls in cliques as senior officers, no meritocracy and no policies or procedures to enforce any proper rules and boundaries. It left me with a love of horses but also a profound sadness, not caused by the horses but the terrible regime of young women bullying girls, not rewarding merit just punishing those already down and in my case a little overweight and lacking confidence at the time. I then developed anorexia aged 15 from which I’ve still not recovered from 23 years later. I hope this has been fixed as I’m not the only one, it damaged many and that impact proportionately may be greater than those who it helped. | Christina Daniel(1): Very disappointing. gmaps_reviews_top3: Steven Duckworth(5): My daughter attended South London Dance Studio for 10 years, and the classes made her what she is today. From her very first class, she was welcomed into a nurturing and professional environment that taught her how to dance and so much more.  Over the years, she trained in multiple dance genres including ballet, modern, tap, and street. The strength, flexibility, and focus she developed translated beautifully into other activities like gymnastics and swimming. The exacting standards showed her what was possible when you apply yourself. The quality of teaching is exceptional. All the teachers we met were passionate, highly skilled, and genuinely invested in each student's progress and well-being.  The studio’s shows are spectacular. From the choreography to the costumes, staging, and overall delivery, the performances are of a professional standard and a real highlight for both dancers and families. Watching my daughter perform in these productions was a joy and a testament to the studio’s commitment to excellence.  I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone looking for top-tier dance training in a supportive, inspiring and inclusive environment. | Hui Lin(1): Before enrolling my 5-year-old daughter, I checked the reviews and noticed quite a few negative comments about the school’s aggressive approach to cancellations and communication. However, I had also heard good things about their teaching staff, so I decided to give it a try and registered my daughter for Saturday lessons. She did enjoy the classes, and I do appreciate the quality of their teaching.  That said, their communication style left a lot to be desired. Emails were overly long, with important information buried in lengthy paragraphs, which I found quite frustrating. At the beginning of the term, I decided to give a polite notification about leaving, citing a busy schedule and the possibility of trying weekday lessons instead. Natasha responded very quickly and, without asking, enrolled my daughter in Monday lessons. I explained that we couldn’t do Mondays, and she immediately asked if she could register her for Wednesday instead. I didn’t reply to this email, meaning I didn’t confirm anything.  To my surprise, after half-term, I received an email stating that my daughter had been registered for Wednesday lessons—even though I never confirmed this. They are now pushing me to pay the invoice for a term we never agreed to, which has left me shocked by how aggressive their approach is. This kind of attitude doesn’t feel appropriate for a dance school, and it’s not something I can trust.  While the teaching quality may be good, the administrative side of things needs a lot of improvement. I can’t recommend the school due to this unpleasant experience. | Elena Andronache(5): My daughter attends this school and she always comes home from lessons feeling inspired to dance even more and work on the corrections she was given. She is always excited to go to lessons and expresses to me how she loves all of her classmates, teachers and dance. She describes her teachers as strict in the best way possible and appreciates them greatly. They are all very very friendly!

name: Surrey Outdoor Learning & Development
description_full:
Surrey Outdoor Learning & Development (SOLD)
The Coach House
Mickleham, Dorking
Surrey RH5 6DQ
Telephone: 01372 378901
Email: outdoor.learning@surreycc.gov.uk.
Website:https://www.surreyoutdoorlearning.uk/
We believe that the outdoors is the place to make unique discoveries.
Surrey Outdoor Learning & Development is committed to achieving positive personal development through outdoor experiences that challenge the mind and body. Through these outdoor experiences, we guide participants to discover more about themselves by learning and developing personal, social, emotional, physical and key skills
We treat each person we work with as an individual with his or her own unique core strengths and limitations. Together, we work to achieve the best each person is capable of, whether this be newfound self-esteem, the ability to communicate effectively with friends or strangers, or the completion of a strenuous uphill hike.
SEN and disability groups
At SOLD, we believe that outdoor experiences can and should benefit any and every person, regardless of physical, mental or psychological needs. From training, planning and delivery, to equipment and building new facilities, inclusion is always front of mind in every aspect of our work.
We believe that the benefit of learning, developing and enjoying oneself in the outdoors are for everyone, no matter their needs. This is especially true for young people for whom opportunities for personal development are vital.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Andrew Wood(5): If you are running an organised camp, then Woodlarks is the place to go. Great facilities for special needs, good camping facilities with onsite tents, showers, and washrooms. The cookhouse is well equipped, and the online management is brilliant. There is a fantastic outdoor heated pool and woodland walk. | Adam Little(5): I recently went camp at woodlarks. Very very nice grounds, some of the best. Great activities all day, ranging from team building to archery and everything between. The entire place was spotless with the help of bins located everywhere in plain sight but not an eye sore. The staff were very knowledgeable, funny and helpful. The only thing I’d change is that there should always be a member of staff available throughout the night. It was harder to deal with things at night with no staff around. | Michael Ọmọlọla(4): Woodlarks Camp Site is, firstly a beautiful place for camping activities. It is situated in a green piece of land. Some of the trees here are over 75 years old. There are tents on site and you can also take your tents along if you wish. There are 5 stars facilities for disabled persons, so rest your mind if there are special needs as the staff in Woodlarks are specially trained and equipped physically, mentally and emotionally to make camping of great pleasure to people with special needs. Nick, Sarah and their team members are super fantastic. We went with a group of students who like to visit Woodlarks every year. And it's an experience I really like even though it was my first ever camping. I like to come back for sure. By the way, Woodlarks is run as a charity so only of their staff is paid. The rest find the place as their life and service to our communities. Bravo to these great team! 👊🏾👊🏾👊🏾

Godstone Group RDA is one of over 500 volunteer groups delivering opportunities for therapy, achievement and enjoyment to people with disabilities. The group was formed in 1977 and operates from Tandridge Priory Stables in Barrow Green Road.
We are a small group which runs several lessons every week during term time for riders with varying disabilities. gmaps_reviews_top3: Olha Yakovleva(5): We recently visited The Royal Mews at Buckingham Palace, and it was a truly memorable experience. It felt incredibly special to be able to see the magnificent royal horses and the stunning historic carriages up close — especially the iconic Gold State Coach used for coronations.  There’s something quite magical about being so close to such an important part of royal tradition and history. My child and I were both fascinated and genuinely impressed.  The staff were absolutely wonderful — warm, friendly, and always happy to answer questions or offer guidance. It was a lovely day out, both educational and inspiring. Highly recommend for anyone with an interest in the British Royal Family or heritage in general. | Angela Criscola(5): This is an amazing Carriage house around the corner from the entrance and exit to Buckingham Palace. Such a treat to see carriages that have carried kings and queens on special occasions. There were also stalls with horses and information to be learned about the staff on hand (who all live upstairs for generations!). Amazing and so worth the cost. You can walk around as long as you like. Kids are welcomed too! Take as many pictures as you like!!! | mongjong59(4): Saw some magnificent carriages here. Absolutely love the black ones (The Diamond Jubilee State Coach, Semi-State Landau, Miniature Landau, Donkey Barouche) and the Gold State Coach. Not crowded as well. To me, this place is so better than the palace itself.
gmaps_editorial_summary: A grand collection of royal transport including horses, cars & the fancy 1762 Gold State Coach.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Aaron Stockwell(4): I’ve been coming to this park since I was little, and it used to be one of my favourite places – peaceful, full of open space, and a perfect spot for family walks, playing, or just relaxing. It had that quiet charm and a real sense of community.  Lately though, it’s definitely changed. Some of the natural feel is gone – more concrete, fewer trees, and it can get a bit crowded or noisy depending on when you go. Don’t get me wrong, it’s still a good place to walk and bring the dog, but it just doesn’t have that same calm vibe it used to. | Dhara Parmar(4): Parking is extortionate if there for less than a few hours. 5.50 regardless of how long or short your stay is. Reservoir is nice to walk/run around. Went for a park run so can't comment on the farm. | Asslinn Abbott(5): We visited for a day trip to the farm and their 100 Aker wood walk. The farm is clean and nicely spaced out. The animals were clearly well fed and looked after well. One of our highlights was the smiling goat! My 4 year old soon was amazed and loved visiting the houses of Eeyore, pooh and the others. He was a exciting walk from one house to the next with cute little stories and the trial book made it a little more fun. He left a letter for pooh and is hoping he will write back as he wasn't home when he visited. Would recommend a day trip here and would likely return. The cafe was reasonably priced and the food was great. The even make home made chips. The staff were all friendly and welcoming across the site as well.
gmaps_editorial_summary: Vast wood- and parkland area with a large lake, rare breeds farm and activities such as pony riding.

name: The Heathway Centre -Dagenham
description_full:
The Heathway Centre,
512a The Heathway,
Dagenham
https://www.lbbd.gov.uk/the-heathway-centre
Tel: 020 8227 5500
email: theheathwaycentre@lbbd.gov.uk
The Heathway Centre is an inclusive resource centre for families with children and young people aged 0 to 17 years (up to their 18th birthday) with additional needs or disabilities. We provide information and advice on a wide range of issues affecting families with children and young people with additional needs or disabilities. We also support families access to specialist training aimed at increasing confidence and awareness for those caring for a child with an additional need.
We’re in a purpose built building with a track and hoist system fitted throughout, wet room, fully equipped kitchen with adjustable height cooker and washing facilities. We have a secure outside play area with a wheelchair accessible swing and mobility adapted bikes.
We provide a variety of adapted resources including a multi sensory room, adjustable cooker, iPads, interactive floor, interactive screen and adapted IT equipment. We also have available a public use computer suite with free internet access.
We can signpost and refer you to specialist local organisations including:
-Carers of Barking and Dagenham
-Sycamore Trust
-Barnardos
-Portage Team
-Disability Service

gmaps_reviews_top3: Tony Witchalls(5): My 11yr old Grandson has been to Stubbers with his school, he had a fantastic time and thoroughly enjoyed his mini adventure break, based on the Ockendon Road, Upminster. It is an expansive site with a very large Lake. Parking was simple with two large car parks. He has participated in a  whole host of activities including climbing, laser tag, water sports like paddle boarding and banana boat. The only criticism my grandson had was the tents were very cold and he couldn’t warm up, even in his sleeping bag with pjs and a hooded onesie on. He has had an experience that I’m sure will stay with him forever. Thank you Oasis Academy Pinewood and thank you to the staff of Stubbers👍 | Anisa Field(5): My child attended a residential trip with his school. I was able to visit prior to the trip and found everything really well set out and activities available for all age ranges. You need to prebook activities though there are some you can book on the day. The timetable for residential stays is great, allowing time for children to get involved and really enjoy themselves whilst enhancing both social and teamwork skills. There's a disco and a good menu. As my child has additional needs, I travelled to collect and deliver them back meaning I needed to come after closing. Staff are very friendly and helpful on all levels and whilst this is quite a journey for us to travel, it's very reasonably priced and a great day out, outside of a residential trip. | arwa b(5): Nice place for a get together. Fun evening spent doing archery. We had booked a 10 ppl group booking and it was a 45 minute slot. Time went by very quickly. may be will book a little longer one next time. Covered picnic huts and lot of play equipment kept the kids and adults busy.
gmaps_editorial_summary: Water sports, climbing, bush skills learning, laser tag and motor sports fun, plus team exercises.

description_full:
The Log Cabin
Contact Details
Tel:020 8840 3400/ 07849 239 794/
07563 580 002
email: info@logcabin.org.uk
https://www.logcabin.org.uk/
The Log Cabin was founded in 1977 by the Mayor of Ealing, to provide a community based, purpose-built adventure playground for children and young people with additional needs or disabilities. It is used by over 350 children and young people every year. We are based at the Log Cabin Children's Centre in Northfield Avenue.
Children and young people with additional needs and disabilities often remain isolated from others and excluded from mainstream facilities. The Log Cabin is inclusive, welcoming mainstream siblings and local children and integrating everyone so that they socialise together, make new friends and form friendship groups, regardless of ability and of whether they have emotional, physical, behavioural or learning disabilities.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Boldizsar Joey Acsai(1): My wife is due today with our second baby. Our 1 1/2 year old boy was sleeping in the stroller (it was his sleeping time). The receptionist didn't let my pregnant wife in for the appointment with the midwife. Saying that stroller is not allowed... Excuse me, but she is due today with a big belly, our boy is sleeping in the stroller and there is the appointment... They supposed to support families and not let them down... So angry and disappointed of Ealing Log Cabin services... Unbelievable how much stress they causing :( | Amy(5): I’m writing a review now as I’ve stayed up lating Googling to see what has become of Log Cabin. My twin sister and I went to Log Cabin in my primary school years, around 2007-2012, and I see a lot has changed since it’s now 2024. When I went there, it was just one building that was a big hall (actually made out of logs), and a little blue-painted art block next to it ran by Kalpana. And that was it, if I remember correctly. It is a key happy memory from my childhood: Kalpana would help us make Diwali pots out of clay, and there was a huge wooden climbing frame with ramps; we would jump off the top level (all supervised, of course), and crawl under the lowest part of the ramp. There was netting underneath it and a big swing (shaped like a sack) at the back that was like a zip wire. This was before Ofsted inspections led to the Log Cabin becoming more of a secure and modern unit for children, rather that the rowdy outdoor climbing and adventure playground it used to be. I remember playing in the sandpit and the wendy house; and they had an awesome tyre swing that swung between two wooden platforms, which was then replaced by a cross-shaped contraption (4 swings that went up and down as well as horizontally). As Log Cabin got redeveloped, they added a sensory room and an indoor softplay. I can’t find any photos online of the old Log Cabin, sadly. The idea of mixing mainstream kids with children with learning difficulties was excellent, and we learnt to mix and play with these children as if they were no different. When our mum arrived we would beg her to let us stay and play for longer.  When my sister and I were there, the staff were Sam, Sara, Kalpana, David, Poonam and Dennis (the Mennis), and that’s all I can remember. Hope they’re all doing well. | Elif Erkoc Gliarmis(1): This charity rents its venue for organisations/events, but they are far from any professionalism. They charge the premium price versus other venues around in Northfields. During our recent event, their staff's behavior was not only unprofessional but also disruptive, casting a shadow over what should have been a joyous occasion. Not recommending hiring their place for any event. AVOID.

description_full:

Challengers Guildford Playcentre
Stoke Park
Guildford
Surrey
GU1 1TU
If you are a new parent, you will first need to register with Challengers. To do that, visit our Challengers Booking Website or call the Booking Line on 01483 230 939. You will then be asked to complete a ‘First Visit’ at Guildford Playscheme – this is so that you can meet the team and see the scheme in action. Once you have attended a First Visit, you will be able to book sessions for all Challengers Playschemes, including Guildford.
full_address: The Guildford Challengers Playscheme runs from our very own Playcentre - a fantastic play space for disabled and non-disabled children aged 4-12 to explore, including a big play floor, soft play area, dining and cooking area for children and an amazing art room. There's also a brand new state of the art multi-sensory room with thousands of lights, sights and sounds controlled by movement touch and voice and a quiet room for stories and cuddles (and the outer wall is covered with synthetic grass)! There is also an outdoor play structure includes swings, roundabouts, games, water and sand play.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Class Rockets(5): My children, aged 7 and 9 (a boy and a girl); have been going to Timbuktu for the past year. They absolutely love going there. It is one of the few activities they do which always gets a thumbs up. In the holidays they can go every day and I keep expecting them to say they want to do something different but they keep wanting to go every day!! Part of that is the exciting activities planned each day, like Easter egg hunting, slime making, go-kart making, the disco. There is a big outdoor play area, with sand and water play, swings, slide and climbing frames . It is absolutely fantastic for them to go there in the evening, where they get lots of exercise and social interaction, compared to sitting at home on their phones or watching telly. They would even rather go there than the park with me because their friends are there and there are more activities. The staff are absolutely lovely, caring people who I feel completely confident to trust my children's well being with.  I feel so lucky to have Timbuktu nearby as it provides everything children need for healthy physical, emotional, social  and even brain  development.    This is especially needed in today's environment of fear about letting children play outside by themselves with their friends. Here they can play with their friends in a safe and stimulating environment. Brilliant! | Nisha Morgan(5): My son  and I attend the Saturday family days on a regular basis, it is a great friendly environment with so much to do from amazing crafts, activities inside and lets not forget the amazing playground. There is so much to do we stay there from start to finish. The staff  there are so helpful, amazing and involve everyone in all activities. the other children who attend are so friendly, kind and caring and are always willing to help.  The best thing about Timbuktu is how children can explore and learn in a safe place and how the centre has a massive community feel which is entirely child lead. my only regret about Timbuktu is not finding it sooner. | Julie Tomlinson(5): Timbuktu is infinity plus infinity plus infinity plus infinity and beyond, fantastic and brilliant,best fun ever. They are the words from my grandchildren 8yrs and 6yrs. They loved making new friends,all the activities,arts n crafts,an outing,BBQ, water slide,sandpit,all the things to ride and climb on and games. All the staff are amazing,so welcoming,friendly and caring for each child's needs. Healthy lunches given and plenty to drink to keep them hydrated. I recommend Timbuktu to any parent or grandparents who are looking for a fun,safe environment for our energetic kids. Thankyou Timbuktu.    JULIE TOMLINSON.XXX

name:
Richmond-
Marble Hill Adventure Playground
description_full:
The adventure playground at Marble Hill Playcentres is completely unique in this borough. With its huge wooden structures and large open spaces, we provide a special place for thousands of children each year.
Marble Hill Adventure Playground
Marble Hill Park,
Twickenham, TW1 2NL
Tel:  0208 891 0641  Playcentre Manager
email: info@marblehillplaycentres.com
Inclusive Play Project
(Age Range : 5 - 15 years)
Running every Saturday morning throughout the year, the Inclusive Play Project offers children with disabilities or additional needs time to explore the Adventure Playground with their families. No bookings are required and the sessions are completely free.
There are playworkers on site throughout to run activities, play games and offer a little respite for those who might need it. We make full use of all of the rope swings, cook together, do some den building, and make potions and other messy concoctions. It’s a dynamic environment where something that starts off as playing with mud can easily turn into some gardening or painting a picture with natural resources.
Over the years the regular parents and carers have developed an active social group, sharing information, advice or just being a friendly ear to listen.

gmaps_reviews_top3: Lawrence Diamond(5): The One O Clock Club and Adventure Playground are two of the best things about living in this part of London. We visit the OOCC nearly every weekend and neither of our children are ever bored. The staff are friendly and help facilitate play in a discreet way so the kids can just explore, and the cafe is fairly priced and well stocked. It's such good value for money and we're so lucky to have it. | Clare Burroughs(5): The one oclock club is like the dream  backgarden but of your best friends house so you don’t have to tidy up! Your children can run wild with no shoes, get wonderfully wet and grubby and generally have a bit of a 90s childhood experience. There a thousand different toys to play and experiment. I’ve got a 4 year old and a 1 year old and it suits them both perfectly. They can explore the space independently and it’s the perfect size for me to keep an eye on them without hovering over them. We love it! | Tatyana Mursalimov(5): My kids (3.5 and 2 years old) love going to the One O’Clock Club (for kids 0-5 years). It is now open till 5pm, giving more time to play after napping. The Playground is very safe - no swings or big slides or high climbing. And there are great play-workers there playing with the kids and doing all sorts of activities. That means I can relax and enjoy a cup of coffee!!! And yes - there is great coffee there now as well!! The kids can play inside and outside, so a great place to come rain or shine. Our favorite so far has been the big sand pit with a parasol over it.  There is also a brilliant Adventure Playground for over 5s, where the kids play independently and the parents are not allowed. You can go in together on the family days (first Saturday of every month) with the younger ones as well. My daughter absolutely loved it!  Disclaimer: I am one of the trustees of the charity that operates the playcentres. So you can see I am very excited about it :)) Come and try yourself!!! And feel free to ask any questions.

name: Pinner - Arts for life
description_full:
The arts for life project is a charity supporting and championing young people and their families’ emotional health and wellbeing through the therapeutic value of the creative arts.
Our projects focus on individuals with complex hidden needs that push them to the margins of society and the community support structure around them to make impactful change within their everyday lives.
We prioritise our work supporting: disadvantaged young people through disability, circumstance, deprivation and their families; marginalized children and adults.
https://www.artsforlifeproject.org/
Tel: 07816 827775
email: enquiries@artsforlifeproject.org
